name: Two Color Chocolate Brownies
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 425°F. Butter the bottom and sides of two 13 x 9 x 2-inch aluminum pans, and dust lightly with flour.
For brownies, melt chocolate, butter, and coffee on top of a double-boiler until smooth.
In an electric mixer bowl, beat eggs, salt, sugar, and extracts until light in color and creamy. Add melted chocolate to the creamed mixture, then mix in the flour and fold in walnut pieces. Pour batter evenly into pans.
Reduce oven temperature to 375°F and bake for 30 minutes. Test doneness with a toothpick (center should come out clean and dry). Remove from oven and refrigerate immediately to cool.
To make icing, melt white chocolate, sugar, and cream on top of a double-boiler until smooth. Pour over cooled brownies and sprinkle walnuts on top. Return to refrigerator for chocolate to set.
Serve at room temperature or freeze in trays or serve individually by wrapping in saran wrap and foil.
